summary: Today's trending signals point to a deepening focus on both practical security tooling and the evolution of AI post-training methods. On the security front, Simonw's star of the `andrew/pycon` repo highlights growing interest in auditing GitHub Actions security across Python packages, a timely concern as supply chain attacks become more sophisticated. Meanwhile, Nathan Lambert's thread on Bluesky is generating significant discussion around on-policy distillation, which he argues is becoming a permanent fixture across instruction tuning, RLHF, DPO, and RLVR—suggesting the field is converging on a core set of training techniques. This dual emphasis on hardening infrastructure and refining alignment methodologies underscores a maturing ecosystem where both safety and performance are being tackled head-on.
